macos: Upload your legally obtained US ROM with Upload SFC, or place it as zelda3.sfc in the Z3R folder.
After venv activation, press the install dependencies button
Install a C compiler and SDL2 development files with your OS package manager before building.
Press the Build assets button to extract assets, compile zelda3_assets.dat, and build the executable.
When it finishes, go back to the home menu to Play or Randomize.
windows: Upload your legally obtained US ROM with Upload SFC, or place it as zelda3.sfc in the Z3R folder.
Make sure MSBuild is installed, or use the Manual install button beside any missing dependency check.
After venv activation, press the install dependencies button
Use Visual Studio Build Tools with Desktop development with C++ or the TCC package route before building.
Press Build assets TCC, or press Build assets Visual Studio when MSBuild is detected.
When it finishes, go back to the home menu to Play or Randomize.
linux: Upload your legally obtained US ROM with Upload SFC, or place it as zelda3.sfc in the Z3R folder.
Press the Create venv button. On Debian/Ubuntu, install venv support first if Python asks for python3-venv.
After venv activation, press the install dependencies button
Make sure the C compiler check is OK, then install SDL2 development files before building. On apt-based Linux/Unix systems, use `sudo apt-get install libsdl2-dev`.
Press the Build assets button to extract assets, compile zelda3_assets.dat, and build the executable.
When it finishes, go back to the home menu to Play or Randomize.
linux_packaged: Upload your legally obtained US ROM with Upload SFC, or place it as zelda3.sfc in the Z3R folder.
Press the Create venv button. On Debian/Ubuntu, install venv support first if Python asks for python3-venv.
After venv activation, press the install dependencies button
Press the Build assets button to extract assets, compile zelda3_assets.dat, and download the matching Z3R executable.
